data.title: Lundin Gold declares $0.30 dividend and new variable quarterly dividend of $0.49 per share
data.description:
• Lundin Gold (OTCQX:LUGDF) declares $0.30/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.23%
  • The declaration also has a new variable quarterly dividend of $0.49 per share.
  • Payable Sept. 25; to shareholders of record Sept. 10; ex-div Sept. 10.
  • See LUGDF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: High Liner Foods Non-GAAP EPS of $0.38
data.description:
• High Liner Foods press release (OTCPK:HLNFF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.38.
  • Sales volume increased by 6% to 54.8 million pounds compared to 51.7 million pounds, while sales increased by 9.8% Y/Y to $239.6 million.

data.title: Singapore Exchange reports FY results
data.description:
• Singapore Exchange  press release (OTCPK:SPXCF): FY2025 adjusted net profit increased 15.9% year-on-year to S$609.5 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was up 16.9% at S$832.0 million, while adjusted earnings per share was 57.0 cents (49.2 cents).
  • Net revenue increased 11.7% Y/Y to S$1,298.2 million, driven by higher revenue from all business segments.

data.title: Stock index futures inch up amid tech gains; tariff concerns linger
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday, amid a rally in tech stocks that boosted market sentiment, even as investors continued to weigh the impact of Donald Trump's tariffs.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.26%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.75%.

Wall Street's major averages on Thursday closed mixed, as positive sentiment sparked by U.S. President Donald Trump's chip tariff exemptions and progress on Russia-Ukraine de-escalation petered out.

"Markets have been mixed over the past 24 hours, with the S&P 500 edging -0.08% lower, partly weighed down by disappointing earnings from Eli Lilly, even as the Nasdaq (+0.35%) and the Mag-7 (+0.40%) reached new all-time highs," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Trump's reciprocal tariffs took effect at midnight on Thursday, and traders will keep a watch on the impact the trade policies have going ahead.

"On the trade front, Trump’s tariffs officially came into effect yesterday, though markets showed little reaction," Reid said.

In other news, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is reportedly gaining momentum in the process to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the central bank.

There is not much economic data on Friday. St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem will speak in an event hosted by Mississippi Valley State University.

data.title: ECN Capital reports Q2 results
data.description:
• ECN Capital press release (OTCPK:ECNCF): Q2 adjusted net income applicable to common shareholders of $10.0 million or $0.04 per share (basic), versus $7.2 million or $0.03 per share (basic) for the previous three-month period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $31.5 million, versus $25.5 million for the previous three-month period and $31.5 million for the prior year comparable period.

data.title: European shares mixed as investors eye corporate results, trade talks
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.05%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.31% 

France (CAC:IND)  +0.12%

The unemployment rate in France was unchanged at 7.5% in Q2 2025, in line with market expectations.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.13% on Friday, on track to close the week with strong gains driven by upbeat corporate earnings and renewed optimism over potential progress in resolving the Ukraine–Russia conflict. 

Investors also weighed changing global trade dynamics after President Trump’s broad retaliatory tariffs took effect Thursday.

Among stocks, Novo Nordisk (NVO) extended the rally, Novartis (NVS) gained, and Siemens went up 1% while SAP lost 1%. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point at 4.25%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.65%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points at 4.57%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
